hero_subtitle: SFTY helps banking operations teams preserve and organize compliance decisions with structured workflows that link provider data, regulatory rules, case evidence, and review history for audit readiness.
trust_card_1_value: Identity checks connected to onboarding decisions
trust_card_2_value: Corporate verification, UBO review and entity risk
trust_card_3_value: Wallet and transaction monitoring in one workflow
trust_card_4_value: Message routing, completeness checks and exception handling
trust_card_5_value: Regulatory changes mapped to internal rule
trust_card_6_value: Decision logs and evidence for every review
problem_title: Audit readiness weakens without structured decision logs
problem_description: Compliance teams struggle to create clear, traceable records of decisions across fragmented provider outputs and manual workflows. This gap slows audit preparation and weakens evidence needed for regulatory, bank or internal reviews.
problem_point_1_value: KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule outputs sit in separate tools
problem_point_2_value: Teams move data between dashboards, spreadsheets and internal notes
problem_point_3_value: Regulatory changes are hard to map into internal workflows
problem_point_4_value: Evidence and decision history are difficult to reconstruct later
solution_description: SFTY connects provider outputs, review actions, decision approvals and evidence preservation into one structured compliance workflow for banks to maintain clear, traceable and audit-ready decision records.
workflow_title: From provider checks to audit-ready decisions
workflow_description: SFTY applies internal rules, routes reviews, opens cases and records decisions with evidence to create auditable compliance workflows for operational teams.
workflow_step_1_description: KYC, KYB, KYT and screening outputs enter SFTY
workflow_step_2_description: Risk rules route checks into the right workflow
workflow_step_3_description: A case opens with customer, wallet and provider context
workflow_step_4_description: Teams review, decide and record the outcome
workflow_step_5_description: Logs, decisions and reports stay ready for audit
why_sfty_title: Beyond fragmented checks. Audit-ready decision logs
why_sfty_description: SFTY connects compliance events into structured decisions, preserved evidence and audit-ready workflows, enabling banks to maintain clear, defensible records without replacing existing providers.
why_sfty_card_1_description: Work above existing KYC, KYB, KYT and Travel Rule tools
why_sfty_card_2_description: Turn disconnected outputs into structured reviews and cases
why_sfty_card_3_description: Preserve evidence, reviewer actions and decision history
why_sfty_card_4_description: Give compliance, risk and leadership one operating view
authority_description: SFTY supports banks with controlled workflows that preserve evidence, connect provider outputs and prepare compliant records for regulatory, audit and due diligence review.
authority_card_1_description: Connect KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening providers into one workflow
authority_card_2_description: Preserve provider outputs, reviewer notes, decisions and supporting records
authority_card_3_description: Prepare clearer compliance evidence for banks, partners and external review
authority_card_4_description: Support private environments, permissions, integrations and internal governance
ask_sfty_placeholder: How can SFTY support audit-ready compliance decision logs
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how decision logs preserve evidence for regulators
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how workflows connect provider checks to case decisions
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY supports operational risk control and audit
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for product, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on provider orchestration, Travel Rule workflows, audit evidence and enterprise compliance operations.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for compliance teams?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides compliance teams a connected workflow layer that connects KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule, screening, cases, decisions and reporting across multiple jurisdictions and providers.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle decision workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY applies internal policies to provider outputs, routes reviews, opens cases, logs decisions and automates workflows to reduce manual workload and improve operational consistency.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves comprehensive audit evidence including case history, decision logs and supporting data to support review readiness and regulatory or audit inspections.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing providers?
faq_4_answer: Yes. SFTY is provider-neutral and integrates outputs from existing KYC, KYB, KYT, Travel Rule and screening vendors into one structured compliance operating layer.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace current compliance providers?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY works above existing providers to connect and structure their outputs rather than replacing them, enabling better control and traceability across compliance workflows.
final_description: Review how SFTY structures decision logs, links provider data and preserves evidence for banking compliance workflows.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
